Entries.Name: Narrator
Entries.Line: It seems that you have walked in on something. A tall, proud Kweebec is pacing up and down the meeting room, yelling at a glowing rune in her hand.

Entries.Name: Business Kweebec
Entries.Line: --that was our rainy day fund! Not a 'buy another car for Finance Kweebec' fund!

Entries.Name: Business Kweebec
Entries.Line: Giving yourself a massive bonus would've been okay five years ago, but we can't afford that right now. Do you understand? We're being attacked on all fronts. Peartree Incorporated's new--

Entries.Name: Narrator
Entries.Line: She pauses, and though you can't hear whomever she's talking to, you can see her whole body tensing while her trek continues. Up and down, up and down -- yet, she somehow doesn't notice you in meantime.

Entries.Name: Business Kweebec
Entries.Line: Our marketing department's produced some pretty concerning charts-- No, no, no, if you can't be bothered to read-- That's what I was saying! Our magic cloud is bleeding out. Peartree put out their own 'solution' with 'better value'--

Entries.Name: Business Kweebec
Entries.Line: You can whine about 'hearsay' all you want, but the fact is that our customers are jumping ship. Over a quarter of Indigo infra will be spun down soon--

Entries.Name: Business Kweebec
Entries.Line: Yes! It is 'insane', but it's also the reality of it. You wouldn't be surprised if you read any of the messages I've sent you.

Entries.Name: Business Kweebec
Entries.Line: What I want to know is how Peartree could've developed their stuff in secret, over all these years, and then have such a successful surprise launch. They didn't even do anything to market it properly. Meanwhile, we've had--

Line.Text: --yes, the campaign is still ongoing. Mhm. And how can these total *aphids* put out something so 'good' that a quarter of magic institutes want to leave a mature, well-established platform?
